params.name: Temple Pediment
params.description: The temple pediment is usually located on the gable part of a temple or important building, in a triangular shape, supported by columns or pilasters. It originated from the Lion Gate form of the Mycenaean era. The temple pediment at the Roman Baths Museum in Bath, UK, is one of the museum's famous collections. It is supported by four fifteen-meter-high Corinthian columns, with the central carving featuring the head of the snake-haired monster Gorgon, symbolizing the temple's majesty. Additionall …
